title: veridict power report
description: JSON output of `veridict power`. Report-only, no input file - a pure calculation from flags. Estimates how many trials --metric winrate/sign-test/elo would need for a target_power probability of reaching a passing verdict, assuming the true effect is exactly assume_effect against a pass bar of min_effect.

schema_version.description: Stays 1 across additive changes; bumps only when a field is removed or renamed.

metric.description: mean-diff is not supported - rejected at the CLI flag-parsing level (a distinct --metric enum from compare's), and by PowerMetric::new for direct library callers.

ci_method.description: Always wilson for metric elo - the same restriction compare --metric elo itself has.

min_effect.description: The pass bar - identical meaning to compare --min-effect/--pass-above.

assume_effect.description: The true effect being powered for. Strictly greater than min_effect - evaluating power with the true effect equal to the pass bar only recovers the interval's own miscoverage at that boundary, not a number that climbs toward target_power with more trials.

target_power.description: Target probability of reaching a passing verdict, assuming the true effect is exactly assume_effect.

achieved_power.description: The real exact power at estimated_trials, always >= target_power - can overshoot the target by a nontrivial margin due to the discrete, non-monotonic ('sawtooth') nature of exact CI methods; see docs/metrics.md's power section.

notes.description: Always includes the 'assumes the true effect is exactly assume_effect' caveat; adds a --paired-by-id caveat when that flag was set.
